title: Genomic DNA Extraction
author: Likhith Chandragiri
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bxeupjew

steps.title: Prepare cell pellet by centrifuging an overnight culture of E
steps.instruction: Prepare cell pellet by centrifuging an overnight culture of E. coli at 4 °C and 7000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00

steps.title: Gently resuspend the cell pellet in...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend the cell pellet in 500 µL of autoclaved MilliQ water by mixing with a pipette and transfer into a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube (MCT) in a laminar flow hood.

steps.title: Add 75 µL of SDS and...
steps.instruction: Add 75 µL of SDS and 3 µL of proteinase K (or how much ever volume is needed to achieve a final concentration of 100 ug/mL).

steps.title: Heat at 95 °C for 00:05:00 to inactivate proteinase K
steps.instruction: Heat at 95 °C for 00:05:00 to inactivate proteinase K.

steps.title: Let the tube cool to Room...
steps.instruction: Let the tube cool to Room temperature Add 5 µL of RNase A (or how much ever volume is needed to achieve a final concentration of 200 ug/mL). Leave the tube at room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Phenol-Chloroform mixture to the tube
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Phenol-Chloroform mixture to the tube. Centrifuge at 10.000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C The contents of the tube should phase separate into three layers: an aqueous layer on top, a viscous jelly-like layer in the middle, and a layer of chloroform at the bottom.

steps.title: Collect the aqueous layer and the...
steps.instruction: Collect the aqueous layer and the jelly-like layer using a cut tube and transfer them into a fresh MCT. Add 1 mL of Phenol-Chloroform mixture to this tube. Centrifuge again at 10.000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C The contents of the tube should phase separate into three layers as before.

steps.title: Collect only the aqueous layer at...
steps.instruction: Collect only the aqueous layer at the top and transfer it into a fresh MCT. Add 160 µL of 3 Molarity (M) Sodium Acetate to this tube. Mix gently.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Isopropanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Isopropanol to the tube and mix gently by inversion till white strands of DNA precipitate out Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C

steps.title: Discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant. Add 1 mL of Chilled 70% Ethanol gently along the walls of the tube, without disturbing the DNA pellet. Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, for 00:10:00 at 4 °C

steps.title: Air-dry the tube till there isn't any ethanol remaining
steps.instruction: Air-dry the tube till there isn't any ethanol remaining.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA pellet gently in...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA pellet gently in 100 µL of autoclaved MilliQ water by mixing with a pipette under a laminar flow hood. Store the suspension in a -30 °C refrigerator.

steps.title: Load 1 µL of autoclaved MilliQ...
steps.instruction: Load 1 µL of autoclaved MilliQ water as blank on NanoDrop and load 1 µL of gDNA suspension as the sample to measure its concentration.

steps.title: Prepare a 1% agarose gel by adding 0
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1% agarose gel by adding 0.5 g of agarose in 50 mL of TAE buffer with 2 µL of ethidium bromide.

steps.title: Load 3 µL of 1kb DNA...
steps.instruction: Load 3 µL of 1kb DNA Ladder into the first lane. Load 1 µL gDNA + 1 µL dye into the second lane. Run the gel. Observe for a single band above the first band of the ladder, close to the well.
